what_it_does: Implements an HTTP speech-to-text ear service on port 2033 using IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1B through HuggingFace transformers. It lazily loads a processor and seq2seq model, exposes a POST /inference multipart endpoint that accepts audio bytes, and returns a JSON {"text": ...} transcription produced via a chat-template prompt with an <|audio|> turn.
observations: Uses sys.path.insert to import voice.ears._stt_service and wav_bytes_to_float32.
Keeps a lazy singleton of processor and model in module globals _proc and _model.
Guards the torch/transformers import and raises a clear install-error message.
Builds a chat-template prompt: [{'role':'user','content':'<|audio|>can you transcribe the speech into text?'}].
Runs greedy generation (do_sample=False, max_new_tokens=200) and strips the generated text.
Selects bfloat16 on CUDA and float32 on CPU based on COMPANY_GRANITE_DEVICE.
Sets the model to eval mode after loading.
Expects mono float32 @ 16k audio via wav_bytes_to_float32.
Serves via _stt_service.serve with a warm=_engine callback.
Docstring warns about CUDA-13 GPU hazard and instructs to set cpu to avoid it.
standouts: Chat-template-based transcription with an explicit <|audio|> token for a multimodal speech LLM.
Explicit fail-loud design: no silent fallback if the GPU fails to load or dependencies are missing.
Dtype selection tied to device to control VRAM usage.
conventions: Lazy singleton initialization for heavy ML resources.
Guarded imports with human-readable RuntimeError messages.
Module-level docstring with install/run instructions and API example.
concerns: No explicit resampling or sample-rate validation in this file; relies entirely on wav_bytes_to_float32.
Hardcoded English transcription task in the chat prompt.
Global singleton is not thread-safe on first load.
DEVICE.startswith('cuda') would mis-handle values like 'cuda:0'? Actually 'cuda:0'.startswith('cuda') is true, so fine.
inputs.to(DEVICE) may move a tensor that was already placed by proc(..., device=DEVICE).
No handling for model.generate exceptions or empty generation.
notes: A self-contained transformers-based STT worker that refuses to degrade silently when GPU or dependencies fail.
questions: What does voice.ears._stt_service.serve provide for routing, concurrency, and error handling?
What exact sampling rate and channel format does wav_bytes_to_float32 produce?
How are concurrent calls to _engine() and transcribe() serialized or protected?
What additional endpoints, if any, does _stt_service expose beyond POST /inference and GET /?
Which exact transformers class layout is expected, and how is the chat-template behavior version-locked?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is an HTTP STT ear using IBM Granite Speech via transformers, with lazy loading and a chat-template generate path.
apparent_themes: HuggingFace transformers as the standard/clean path
Minimal HTTP wrapper around a generative speech model
intention_signals: Provide a robust, isolated STT microservice that avoids the NeMo/CUDA-13 hazard by using HuggingFace transformers and refuses silent wrong transcripts or silent GPU failures.
novelty: Treats speech transcription as a multimodal LLM chat task using an explicit <|audio|> turn rather than a conventional ASR decoder pipeline.
contribution: Encapsulates a Granite Speech STT inference worker: model loading, dtype selection, chat-template prompt construction, greedy generation, and a simple HTTP transcribe endpoint, usable as one ear in a larger voice system.
summary_for_embedding: company/voice/ears/granite.py is a HuggingFace-transformers HTTP STT service using IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1B that transcribes uploaded WAV audio via a chat-template prompt with an <|audio|> token and returns JSON text.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a standalone, fail-loud speech-to-text ear that loads IBM Granite Speech lazily and exposes a simple HTTP transcription endpoint without relying on NeMo.

symbols.does: Lazily loads and returns the HuggingFace processor and Granite speech model singleton.
symbols.description: On first call it imports torch and transformers, constructs AutoProcessor and AutoModelForSpeechSeq2Seq from COMPANY_GRANITE_MODEL, selects bfloat16 on CUDA or float32 on CPU, maps the model to COMPANY_GRANITE_DEVICE, sets eval mode, and caches the pair in module globals for reuse.

symbols.does: Converts WAV bytes to a float32 tensor and returns a transcription dict via Granite generate.
symbols.description: Calls _engine(), decodes the input audio to mono float32 samples, builds a chat-template prompt containing an <|audio|> turn, runs greedy model.generate for up to 200 new tokens, batch-decodes and strips the generated continuation, and returns {"text": text}.
